assertion_results.text: Step 0 extracts the Embargo policy URL from the Security Configuration as an optional field without raising an error (§1.71 — backward compatible extraction)
assertion_results.evidence: In embargo-check.md (Step 1.7), the Configuration Check section extracts the URL: 'Embargo policy URL is configured in Security Configuration: Embargo policy URL: https://example.com/security/embargo-policy' and then states 'Result: Embargo check is enabled (URL is configured). Proceeding to severity evaluation.' The conditional phrasing ('is configured') demonstrates optional-field treatment — the check evaluates whether the URL is present or not, and no error is raised. The extraction proceeds smoothly as a backward-compatible optional field.

assertion_results.text: Step 1.7 evaluates the CVE severity — CVSS 7.5 (High) meets the Critical/Important threshold (CVSS >= 7.0) and triggers the embargo warning gate (§1.70)
assertion_results.evidence: embargo-check.md is explicitly labeled 'Step 1.7 -- Embargo Check: CVE-2026-31812' and contains a Severity Evaluation table: CVE ID = CVE-2026-31812, CVSS score = 7.5, Severity = High, Trigger threshold = 'Critical or Important (CVSS >= 7.0)', Threshold met? = 'YES -- 7.5 >= 7.0'. The gate is then triggered and a warning is presented.

assertion_results.text: Step 1.7 presents a warning gate to the engineer including the CVE ID (CVE-2026-31812), severity level, a statement about embargo risk, and a link to the configured embargo policy URL (https://example.com/security/embargo-policy) (§1.70)
assertion_results.evidence: embargo-check.md presents a warning gate containing all required elements: (1) CVE ID: 'EMBARGO CHECK -- CVE-2026-31812'; (2) Severity level: '(High severity)'; (3) Embargo risk statement: 'High-severity vulnerabilities may be under embargo. Before proceeding, verify with your security team that this CVE is cleared for public triage.'; (4) Embargo policy URL: 'Embargo policy: https://example.com/security/embargo-policy'.

assertion_results.text: Step 1.7 requires explicit engineer confirmation (Yes/No) before proceeding to Step 2 — the gate is a confirmation prompt, not an informational message (§1.70)
assertion_results.evidence: embargo-check.md presents 'Proceed with triage? (Yes / No)' as a confirmation prompt. The Gate Behavior section explicitly conditions further execution on the response: 'If the engineer responds "Yes": Proceed to Step 2 (Version Impact Analysis) as normal' and 'If the engineer responds "No": Stop execution immediately. Inform the user to check embargo status with their security team before re-running triage. Do not proceed to Step 2.' This is clearly a blocking confirmation gate, not an informational message.

assertion_results.text: The embargo warning gate does NOT trigger for Low or Moderate severity CVEs (CVSS < 7.0) — it is skipped silently when severity is below threshold (§1.70)
assertion_results.evidence: The outputs only demonstrate the above-threshold case (CVSS 7.5, High). While embargo-check.md defines the threshold as 'CVSS >= 7.0' and evaluates 'Threshold met? YES -- 7.5 >= 7.0', there is no explicit statement about what happens when the threshold is NOT met. The outputs do not state that the gate is skipped silently for CVSS < 7.0, nor is there any below-threshold scenario demonstrated. No evidence in any output file addresses the behavior for Low or Moderate severity CVEs.
